en.name: Prothrombin ratio in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The prothrombin ratio is a transformation of the prothrombin time (PT) expressed as a percentage of the activity of pooled normal plasma. It is one of the standard outputs of a coagulation analyser, alongside the PT in seconds. A healthy patient has a prothrombin ratio of ~100 %; lower values indicate longer clotting times. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a coagulation analyser on platelet poor plasma (PPP). Results are reported in **%** [2]. **Note: prothrombin ratio vs PT vs INR.** The prothrombin ratio (this concept set), the PT in seconds, and the INR are three different representations of the same underlying physical measurement, with different normalisations: - **Prothrombin ratio** — percentage of pooled normal plasma activity (this concept set). - **PT (seconds)** — raw measurement; depends on the laboratory's thromboplastin reagent. Captured by a separate concept set, "Prothrombin time in blood". - **INR** — dimensionless, reagent-independent transformation. Used for vitamin K antagonist (VKA) monitoring. Captured by a separate concept set, "INR in blood". The three representations are **not interconvertible** without the laboratory's local calibration curve. The data dictionary keeps them addressable independently — analyses needing a homogeneous variable should pick one representation rather than merging across. ## Included Concepts The set contains one LOINC "Lab Test" concept: - *3033658 / 5894-1 — Prothrombin time (PT) actual/Normal*: result expressed as a percentage of pooled normal plasma activity. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`%`** (concept 8554 — percent). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The following LOINC concepts that exist for PT actual/Normal variants are deliberately not part of this set: - **PIVKA-sensitive / PIVKA-insensitive variants** (`3039709 / 52753-1`, `3042131 / 52761-4`, `3042392 / 52752-3`, `3041830 / 52760-6`): specialised assays for vitamin K antagonist monitoring, not the routine prothrombin ratio. - **Factor substitution / control / mixing study** variants (e.g. `1091858 / 105049-1`): research context, not routine measurement. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "prothrombin ratio" or "prothrombin index" with the value as a percentage of normal: `3033658 / 5894-1 — Prothrombin time (PT) actual/Normal`. - **Source reports PT in seconds**: do **not** map to this concept set. PT in seconds has its own concept set, "Prothrombin time in blood". - **Source reports INR**: do **not** map to this concept set. INR has its own concept set, "INR in blood". - **Do not confuse with**: PT in seconds, INR, aPTT, thrombin time, Reptilase time. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 5894-1 — Prothrombin time (PT) actual/Normal. Available: https://loinc.org/5894-1/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
